title: Sharp norm inflation for 3D Navier-Stokes equations in supercritical spaces

thm:1.1.summary: NSE is strongly ill-posed in Ḃˢₚ,$q(\mathbb{R}^3)$ for any s,p,q with -3 < s - 3/p < -1: for any $\varepsilon>0$ there exists $t*\le\varepsilon$ and a smooth solution u with ||u₀||_{Ḃˢₚ,$1} \le \varepsilon$ that develops norm inflation ||u(t*)||_{Ḃˢₚ,$\infty} \ge \varepsilon$⁻^1 at time t*. In particular the initial data can be taken in $C^\infty_c$.

rem:1.2.summary: Notes that the upper bound s = -1 + 3/p is sharp (solution map continuous for small data in $B^{-1+3/p}_{p,\infty})$, that thm:1.1 extends Bourgain-Pavlović Ḃ⁻$^1\infty,\infty$ norm inflation, and that thm:1.1 implies the same strong ill-posedness in supercritical Sobolev spaces Ẇˢ,ᵖ.

thm:1.3.summary: Vorticity norm inflation: for any M>0 there exists t*>0 and a smooth NSE solution with u₀$\inC^\infty_c$ such that $|\omega(t*)|_{L^\infty}/|\omega$₀$|_{L^\infty} \ge$ M. In particular the initial data can be arbitrarily small in any supercritical homogeneous Sobolev/Besov space.

lem:1.4.summary: Besov interpolation inequality (from Bahouri-Chemin-Danchin): for s₁ < s₂ and $\alpha$ interpolation parameter, |f|_{Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $|f|^\alpha_{$Ḃˢ^1ₚ,$\infty} |f|^{1-\alpha}_{$Ḃˢ^2ₚ,$\infty}$ where s $= \alphas$₁ $+ (1-\alpha)s$₂.

prop:1.5.summary: Smooth NSE solution Sobolev propagation (from Kato-Ponce): if $|\nablau|_{L^\infty([0,t$₀];$L^\infty)} \le$ M then $|u(t)|_{W^{k,p}} \le |u$₀$|_{W^{k,p}} exp(C_{k,p}$ M t) for all $t\in[0,t$₀], with universal constants independent of M and t₀.

prop:2.1.summary:
Main construction: for given s,p in the range of thm:1.1 and any $\varepsilon>0, \mu,
u\ge1$ satisfying eq:2.13, there exists a smooth axisymmetric approximate solution ū: $[0,\infty)	imes\mathbb{R}^3	o\mathbb{R}^3$ and critical time $t*(\mu,
u,\varepsilon)$ such that ū satisfies NSE with error Ē with regularity estimates (2.1) and error decay (2.3) in $W^{k,q}$ norms depending on $\mu^{k-s}$ and $
u^{1/p}$.

lem:2.2.summary: Pressure construction for stationary Euler subsystem: there exists smooth pressure p̄(r,z) such that the radial-z components (ū_r, ū_{z,p}) satisfy a stationary Euler equation (2.21) on $\mathbb{R}$⁺$\times\mathbb{R}$, and the rotation formula (2.22): (ū_r $\partial_r +$ ū$_{z,p} \partial_z)G = \varepsilon^2\mu^{-s+2/p}\nu^{1/p} f'(\rho)/\rho \cdot \partial_\varphi$ G for any smooth G.

lem:2.3.summary: Sobolev/Besov regularity estimates for ū: |ū$|_{L^\infty([0,t*]$; $W^{k,q})} \le C_{\varepsilon,k,q} \mu^{k-s}(\mu^{2/p-2/q} \nu^{1/p-1/q})$, with constant independent of $\mu,\nu$.

prop:3.1.summary: Norm inflation for ū in the case s>0: (1) initial data is small: |ū₀|_{Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $\varepsilon^2$; (2) at critical time t* defined in eq:2.14, ū exhibits Ḃˢₚ,$\infty$ inflation: |ū(t*)|_{Ḃˢₚ,$\infty}$ ≳ $\varepsilon$⁻^2. Both bounds independent of $\varepsilon,\mu,\nu$.

lem:3.2.summary: Core oscillation estimate: for twisted profile G $= h(\varphi - \zeta(\rho))g(\mu\rho)$ with $\zeta(\rho) = \varepsilon^{-N}\mu$⁻$^1\rho$⁻^1, the $L^q$ radial derivative satisfies $|\partial_\rho$ᵏ $G|_{L^q(\mathbb{R}^3)}$ ~ $\varepsilon^{-Nk} \mu^{k-2/q} \nu^{-1/q}$ for $\varepsilon$ small enough. This twisting mechanism is the engine of the norm inflation.

lem:3.3.summary: Vorticity lower bound: under prop:3.1 setting, $|\omega$̄$(t*)|_{L^q}$ ≳ $\varepsilon$⁻$^2 \mu^{1-s} \mu^{2/p-2/q} \nu^{1/p-1/q}$, where $\omega$̄ $= \nabla\times$ū is the vorticity of the approximate solution.

prop:4.1.summary: Norm inflation for ū in the case s<0: same structure as prop:3.1 — small initial data |ū₀|_{Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $\varepsilon^2 (proved$ by decomposing ū into radial, z, and $\theta$ components via lem:4.2-4.5) and inflation |ū(t*)|_{Ḃˢₚ,$\infty}$ ≳ $\varepsilon$⁻^2 at critical time t*.

lem:4.2.summary: Radial component initial data is small: under prop:4.1 setting, |ū_r $e_r|_{$Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $\varepsilon^2$. This bounds the initial Besov norm contribution from the radial part of the axisymmetric field.

lem:4.3.summary: z-component initial data is small: under prop:4.1 setting, |ū_z $e_z|_{$Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $\varepsilon^2$. Bounds initial Besov norm from the vertical component.

lem:4.4.summary: $\theta-component$ initial data is small: $|u_{0,\theta} e_\theta|_{$Ḃˢₚ,1} ≲ $\varepsilon^2$. Together with lem:4.2 and lem:4.3 this establishes the small initial data part of prop:4.1.

lem:4.5.summary: $\theta-component$ at critical time is large: |ū$_\theta(t*) e_\theta|_{$Ḃˢₚ,1} ≳ $\varepsilon$⁻^2 for $\varepsilon\le\varepsilon$₀. This is the source of the norm inflation in the s<0 case, via the twisting mechanism from lem:3.2.

prop:5.1.summary: Approximation of true NSE solution: for $\mu\ge\mu_\varepsilon$ sufficiently large, the maximal existence time T satisfies t*<T (the solution exists past t*), and |u-ū$|_{L^\infty([0,t*]$; Ḃᵏ$q,\infty)} \le C_{k,\varepsilon}(\mu$⁻$^1\nu)^\gamma \mu^{k-s} \mu^{2/p-2/q} \nu^{1/p-1/q}$ with $\gamma=9/10 (q\le2)$ or 4/10 (q>2). True solution u is $O(\mu^{-\gamma})-close$ to ū, so inflation transfers.

lem:5.2.summary:
Bootstrap $H^k$ estimate for difference w=u-ū: under bootstrap assumption (†), $|
abla$ᵏ$w(t)|_{L^2} \le C_{\varepsilon,k}(\mu$⁻$^1
u)^{\delta_k} \mu^{k-s} \mu^{2/p-1} 
u^{1/p-1/2}$ for $\delta_k = (9+10^{-k})/10 > 0$. This is the core energy estimate that closes the bootstrap in prop:5.1.

edges.note: thm:1.1 s>0 case: norm inflation of true solution u follows from inflation of ū (prop:3.1) plus approximation (prop:5.1)
thm:1.1 s<0 case: norm inflation of u follows from inflation of ū (prop:4.1) plus approximation (prop:5.1)
thm:1.1 uses prop:5.1 to transfer norm inflation from approximate solution ū to true solution u
thm:1.3 vorticity inflation follows from lem:3.3 (vorticity bound on ū) combined with prop:5.1 approximation
thm:1.3 uses prop:5.1 to show vorticity of u tracks that of ū, so inflation transfers
prop:5.1 uses the approximate solution ū and its error estimate Ē from prop:2.1 to control the difference w=u-ū
prop:5.1 is proved by closing the bootstrap via the $H^k$ energy estimate lem:5.2
prop:5.1 uses the Sobolev propagation estimate prop:1.5 for the bootstrap in the high-regularity regime
lem:5.2 bootstrap estimate uses the error bound |Ē$|_{W^{k,q}}$ from prop:2.1 as the forcing for the w=u-ū equation
prop:3.1 norm inflation of ū for s>0 invokes the specific structure and parameters of the approximate solution from prop:2.1
prop:3.1 uses lem:3.2 (oscillation estimate for twisted profile) to compute the Besov norm inflation at t*
prop:3.1 references lem:3.3 for the vorticity lower bound as a consequence
prop:4.1 norm inflation of ū for s<0 invokes the approximate solution structure from prop:2.1
prop:4.1 smallness of initial data (s<0 case) uses lem:4.2 for the radial component bound
prop:4.1 uses lem:4.3 for z-component initial data bound
prop:4.1 uses lem:4.4 for $\theta-component$ initial data bound
prop:4.1 norm inflation at t* uses lem:4.5: $\theta-component$ grows ≳ $\varepsilon$⁻^2 due to twisting
prop:2.1 uses the pressure construction lem:2.2 (stationary Euler for the radial-z subsystem) to build ū with the required axisymmetric structure
prop:2.1 uses the regularity estimates of lem:2.3 to verify the $W^{k,q}$ bounds (2.1) on ū
lem:3.2 uses the rotation formula (2.22) from lem:2.2 — the twisting $\zeta(\rho)$ in the profile G arises from the Euler flow structure of ū
lem:3.3 vorticity lower bound uses the oscillation estimate lem:3.2 to compute $|\nabla\times$ū$|_{L^q}$ at time t*
lem:$4.5 \theta-component$ inflation uses the same twisted profile oscillation mechanism as lem:3.2, adapted to the $\theta-component$ Besov norm
lem:4.2 uses the $W^{k,q}$ estimates of lem:2.3 to bound the radial component initial data in Ḃˢₚ,1
lem:4.3 uses lem:2.3 estimates for z-component bound
rem:1.2 annotates and contextualizes thm:1.1 — sharpness at s=-1+3/p, comparison to Bourgain-Pavlović

approximate_construction.description: Build smooth axisymmetric approximate solution ū satisfying NSE with small error, with precise regularity and rotation structure

inflation_s_positive.description: Prove norm inflation for ū in the s>0 case via oscillatory twisted profile

inflation_s_negative.description: Prove norm inflation for ū in the s<0 case by decomposing into axisymmetric components

approximation_and_transfer.description: Show true NSE solution u tracks ū closely enough that inflation transfers from ū to u

main_results_and_tools.description: Main theorems and auxiliary embedding/propagation tools

MR4462623.title: Sharp nonuniqueness for the Navier-Stokes equations
MR4462623.note: Direct predecessor by same author; CL2022 proves non-uniqueness of weak solutions in $L^p_t L^\infty$, Luo2025 proves norm inflation in Ḃˢₚ,q supercritical spaces

MR3898708.title: Nonuniqueness of weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equation
MR3898708.note: Foundational convex integration non-uniqueness result; cited as ancestor of the non-uniqueness program

2112.03116.title: Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of the forced Navier-Stokes equations
2112.03116.note: Leray non-uniqueness via forced NSE (ABC2022); closely related program, pending queue priority 27

2503.14699.title: Non-Uniqueness of Smooth Solutions of the Navier-Stokes Equations from Critical Data
2503.14699.note: Related 2025 result on smooth non-uniqueness from critical data; same landscape as this paper

2404.07813.title: Illposedness of incompressible fluids in supercritical Sobolev spaces
2404.07813.note: Companion paper by same author (2024); Luo2025 (this paper) is cited as sharper follow-up. Not indexed in ScienceStack.

MR2473255.title: Ill-posedness of the Navier-Stokes equations in a critical space in 3D
MR2473255.note: Original Ḃ⁻$^1\infty,\infty$ norm inflation for NSE (Bourgain-Pavlović 2008). Thm:1.1 extends this result to the full supercritical Besov range.

MR2768550.title: Fourier analysis and nonlinear partial differential equations
MR2768550.note: Besov space textbook; lem:1.4 is [Proposition 2.22] from this book

MR951744.title: Commutator estimates and the Euler and Navier-Stokes equations
MR951744.note: Kato-Ponce 1988; prop:1.5 is [Proposition 4.2] from this paper
